words.definitionEn: opposite to what has been mentioned
words.exampleEn: to travel in the reverse direction

words.definitionEn: the opposite of what has just been mentioned
words.exampleEn: This problem is the reverse of the previous one.

words.definitionEn: to change something completely so that it is the opposite of what it was before
words.exampleEn: Falling birth rates may reverse the trend towards early retirement.

words.definitionEn: an improvement in the condition or strength of something
words.exampleEn: the revival of trade

words.definitionEn: to become, or to make somebody/something become, conscious or healthy and strong again
words.exampleEn: The flowers soon revived in water.

words.definitionEn: connected with political revolution
words.exampleEn: a revolutionary leader

words.definitionEn: speech or writing that is intended to influence people, but that is not completely honest or sincere
words.exampleEn: the rhetoric of political slogans

words.definitionEn: a gun with a long barrel which you hold to your shoulder to fire
words.exampleEn: a hunting rifle

words.definitionEn: a large round container, usually made of wood or metal, with flat ends and, usually, curved sides
words.exampleEn: a beer/wine barrel

words.definitionEn: a situation in which a group of people behave in a violent way in a public place, often as a protest
words.exampleEn: One prison guard was killed when a riot broke out in the jail.

words.definitionEn: to tear something or to become torn, often suddenly or violently
words.exampleEn: rip (something), I ripped my jeans on the fence.

words.definitionEn: responsible for your decisions or actions and expected to explain them when you are asked
words.exampleEn: The state spends taxpayers’ money and should be held accountable.

words.definitionEn: a series of actions that are always performed in the same way, especially as part of a religious ceremony
words.exampleEn: religious rituals

words.definitionEn: strong and healthy
words.exampleEn: She was almost 90, but still very robust.

words.definitionEn: to move gently backwards and forwards or from side to side; to make somebody/something move in this way
words.exampleEn: (+ adv./prep.), The boat rocked from side to side in the waves.

words.definitionEn: a long straight piece of wood, metal or glass
words.exampleEn: The concrete is reinforced with steel rods.

words.definitionEn: to move or turn around a central fixed point; to make something do this
words.exampleEn: Stay well away from the helicopter when its blades start to rotate.

words.definitionEn: the action of an object moving in a circle around a central fixed point
words.exampleEn: the daily rotation of the earth on its axis

words.definitionEn: an official decision made by somebody in a position of authority, especially a judge
words.exampleEn: The court will make its ruling on the case next week.

words.definitionEn: a piece of information, or a story, that people talk about, but that may not be true
words.exampleEn: to start/spread a rumour

words.definitionEn: to dismiss somebody from a job
words.exampleEn: She was sacked for refusing to work on Sundays.

words.definitionEn: connected with God or a god; considered to be holy
words.exampleEn: a sacred image/shrine/temple

words.definitionEn: the fact of giving up something important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important; something that you give up in this way
words.exampleEn: The makers of the product assured us that there had been no sacrifice of quality.

words.definitionEn: to give up something that is important or valuable to you in order to get or do something that seems more important for yourself or for another person
words.exampleEn: sacrifice something for somebody/something, She sacrificed everything for her children.

words.definitionEn: a person that the Christian Church recognizes as being very holy, because of the way they have lived or died
words.exampleEn: St John

words.definitionEn: an official order that limits trade, contact, etc. with a particular country, in order to make it do something, such as obeying international law
words.exampleEn: Trade sanctions were imposed against any country that refused to sign the agreement.

words.definitionEn: the right to influence something by giving your opinion before a decision is made
words.exampleEn: say (in something), We had no say in the decision to sell the company.

words.definitionEn: spread far apart over a wide area or over a long period of time
words.exampleEn: a few scattered settlements

words.definitionEn: having doubts that a claim or statement is true or that something will happen
words.exampleEn: She looked highly sceptical.

words.definitionEn: the lowest tone or part in music, for instruments or voices
words.exampleEn: He always plays his stereo with the bass turned right up.

words.definitionEn: the opportunity or ability to do or achieve something
words.exampleEn: scope for something, There's still plenty of scope for improvement.

words.definitionEn: a thin pointed piece of metal like a nail with a raised spiral line (called a thread) along it and a line or cross cut into its head. Screws are turned and pressed into wood, metal, etc. with a screwdriver in order to fasten two things together.
words.exampleEn: One of the screws is loose.

words.definitionEn: to fasten one thing to another or make something tight with a screw or screws
words.exampleEn: The bookcase is screwed to the wall.

words.definitionEn: careful and complete examination
words.exampleEn: Her argument doesn't really stand up to scrutiny.

words.definitionEn: an official design or mark, stamped on a document to show that it is real and carries the authority of a particular person or organization
words.exampleEn: The letter bore the president's seal.

words.definitionEn: to hit a ball with a bat, especially in a game of baseball or cricket
words.exampleEn: He bats very well.

words.definitionEn: to close an envelope, etc. by sticking the edges of the opening together
words.exampleEn: Make sure you've signed the cheque before sealing the envelope.

words.definitionEn: not connected with spiritual or religious matters
words.exampleEn: secular music

words.definitionEn: in a way that appears to be true but may in fact not be
words.exampleEn: a seemingly stupid question

words.definitionEn: a part of something that is separate from the other parts or can be considered separately
words.exampleEn: She cleaned a small segment of the painting.

words.definitionEn: to take somebody/something in your hand suddenly and using force
words.exampleEn: seize something from somebody, She tried to seize the gun from him.

words.definitionEn: not often
words.exampleEn: He had seldom seen a child with so much talent.

words.definitionEn: affecting or involving only a small number of people or things from a larger group
words.exampleEn: the selective breeding of cattle

words.definitionEn: a member of a senate
words.exampleEn: Senator McCarthy

words.definitionEn: a feeling that you get when something affects your body
words.exampleEn: a tingling/burning sensation

words.definitionEn: the ability to understand other people’s feelings
words.exampleEn: sensitivity to the needs of children

words.definitionEn: a feeling or an opinion, especially one based on emotions
words.exampleEn: the spread of nationalist sentiments

words.definitionEn: the act of separating people or things; the state of being separate
words.exampleEn: separation from somebody/something, the state’s eventual separation from the federation

words.definitionEn: doing the same thing in the same way several times; done in the same way several times
words.exampleEn: a serial rapist

words.definitionEn: a place where a battle is being fought or has been fought
words.exampleEn: heavy casualties on the battlefield

words.definitionEn: a way of organizing something; a system
words.exampleEn: I've only been here a couple of weeks and I don't really know the set-up.

words.definitionEn: an official agreement that ends an argument between two people or groups
words.exampleEn: to negotiate a peace settlement

words.definitionEn: to gradually get more and more of something over a period of time
words.exampleEn: I seem to have accumulated a lot of books.

words.definitionEn: a part of the sea, or of a large lake, partly surrounded by a wide curve of the land
words.exampleEn: the Bay of Bengal

words.definitionEn: the feelings and activities connected with a person’s sexual desires
words.exampleEn: He was confused about his sexuality.

words.definitionEn: an owner of shares in a company or business
words.exampleEn: the major shareholders in the company

words.definitionEn: to suddenly break into small pieces; to make something suddenly break into small pieces
words.exampleEn: shatter (into something), He dropped the vase and it shattered into pieces on the floor.

words.definitionEn: to get rid of something that is no longer wanted
words.exampleEn: The factory is shedding a large number of jobs.

words.definitionEn: used to emphasize the size, degree or amount of something
words.exampleEn: The area is under threat from the sheer number of tourists using it.

words.definitionEn: ships in general or considered as a group
words.exampleEn: The canal is open to shipping.

words.definitionEn: a line of light, electromagnetic waves or particles
words.exampleEn: narrow beams of light/sunlight

words.definitionEn: the part that grows up from the ground when a plant starts to grow; a new part that grows on plants or trees
words.exampleEn: new green shoots

words.definitionEn: to become smaller, especially when washed in water that is too hot; to make clothes, cloth, etc. smaller in this way
words.exampleEn: My sweater shrank in the wash.

words.definitionEn: to raise your shoulders and then drop them to show that you do not know or care about something
words.exampleEn: Sam shrugged and said nothing.

words.definitionEn: an act or the sound of sighing
words.exampleEn: to give/heave/let out a sigh

words.definitionEn: to take and then let out a long deep breath that can be heard, to show that you are disappointed, sad, tired, etc.
words.exampleEn: He sighed deeply at the thought.

words.definitionEn: an animal, especially one that is large or dangerous, or one that is unusual
words.exampleEn: wild/savage/ferocious beasts

words.definitionEn: to create particular conditions that exist in real life using computers, models, etc., usually for study or training purposes
words.exampleEn: Computer software can be used to simulate conditions on the seabed.

words.definitionEn: a situation in which a particular set of conditions is created artificially in order to study or experience something that could exist in reality
words.exampleEn: a computer simulation of how the planet functions

words.definitionEn: at the same time as something else
words.exampleEn: The game will be broadcast simultaneously on TV and radio.

words.definitionEn: an offence against God or against a religious or moral law
words.exampleEn: to commit a sin

words.definitionEn: the process of gradually increasing or getting more and more of something over a period of time
words.exampleEn: the accumulation of wealth

words.definitionEn: in a particular place or position
words.exampleEn: My bedroom was situated on the top floor of the house.

words.definitionEn: a simple picture that is drawn quickly and does not have many details
words.exampleEn: The artist is making sketches for his next painting.

words.definitionEn: to move forwards lightly and quickly making a little jump with each step
words.exampleEn: She skipped happily along beside me.

words.definitionEn: to shut, or to make something shut, with a lot of force, making a loud noise
words.exampleEn: I heard the door slam behind him.

words.definitionEn: to hit somebody/something with the flat part of your hand
words.exampleEn: She slapped his face hard.

words.definitionEn: to make a long cut with a sharp object, especially in a violent way
words.exampleEn: slash something, Someone had slashed the tyres on my car.

words.definitionEn: the state of being forced to work as a slave
words.exampleEn: to be sold into slavery

words.definitionEn: a long, narrow opening, into which you put or fit something
words.exampleEn: to put some coins in the slot

words.definitionEn: to break something, or to be broken, violently and noisily into many pieces
words.exampleEn: Several windows had been smashed.

words.definitionEn: to break something suddenly with a sharp noise; to be broken in this way
words.exampleEn: snap something, The wind had snapped the tree in two.

words.definitionEn: to put something in liquid for a time so that it becomes completely wet; to become completely wet in this way
words.exampleEn: soak something (in something), I usually soak the beans overnight.

words.definitionEn: if the value, amount or level of something soars, it rises very quickly
words.exampleEn: soaring costs/prices/temperatures

words.definitionEn: connected with socialism
words.exampleEn: socialist beliefs

words.definitionEn: only; single
words.exampleEn: the sole surviving member of the family

words.definitionEn: only; not involving somebody/something else
words.exampleEn: She was motivated solely by self-interest.

words.definitionEn: a lawyer who prepares legal documents, for example for the sale of land or buildings, advises people on legal matters, and can speak for them in some courts of law
words.exampleEn: Her first step was to contact a solicitor for advice.

words.definitionEn: support by one person or group of people for another because they share feelings, opinions, aims, etc.
words.exampleEn: community solidarity

words.definitionEn: done by one person alone, without anyone helping them
words.exampleEn: his first solo flight

words.definitionEn: a piece of music, dance or entertainment performed by only one person
words.exampleEn: The song features an extended guitar solo.

words.definitionEn: sensible; that you can rely on and that will probably give good results
words.exampleEn: He's a person of very sound judgement.

words.definitionEn: complete power to govern a country
words.exampleEn: The country claimed sovereignty over the island.

words.definitionEn: advertising material sent by email to people who have not asked for it; advertising material on the internet that is not wanted
words.exampleEn: to send/block spam

words.definitionEn: the length of time that something lasts or is able to continue
words.exampleEn: I worked with him over a span of six years.

words.definitionEn: to last all through a period of time or to cover the whole of it
words.exampleEn: His acting career spanned 55 years.

words.definitionEn: to make something such as time or money available to somebody or for something, especially when it requires an effort for you to do this
words.exampleEn: spare something/somebody, I'd love to have a break, but I can't spare the time just now.

words.definitionEn: to cause something to start or develop, especially suddenly
words.exampleEn: spark something, The proposal would spark a storm of protest around the country.

words.definitionEn: designed or developed for a particular purpose or area of knowledge
words.exampleEn: specialized equipment

words.definitionEn: a detailed description of how something is, or should be, designed or made
words.exampleEn: the technical specifications of the new model (= of car)

words.definitionEn: a small amount of something that shows what the rest of it is like
words.exampleEn: Astronauts have brought back specimens of rock from the moon.

words.definitionEn: two lenses in a frame that rests on the nose and ears. People wear spectacles in order to be able to see better or to protect their eyes from bright light.
words.exampleEn: a pair of spectacles

words.definitionEn: a band of coloured lights in order of their wavelengths, as seen in a rainbow and into which light may be separated
words.exampleEn: A spectrum is formed by a ray of light passing through a prism.

words.definitionEn: a short period of time during which something lasts
words.exampleEn: We had a spell of warm weather in April.

words.definitionEn: a solid figure that is completely round, with every point on its surface at an equal distance from the centre
words.exampleEn: The Earth is not a perfect sphere.

words.definitionEn: in order to help somebody
words.exampleEn: He began a petition in behalf of the farmers.

words.definitionEn: a very fast turning movement
words.exampleEn: Only in the last 50 years have we had clocks accurate enough to measure changes in the earth's spin.

words.definitionEn: to turn round and round quickly; to make something do this
words.exampleEn: (+ adv./prep.), The plane was spinning out of control.

words.definitionEn: the row of small bones that are connected together down the middle of the back
words.exampleEn: A shiver went down my spine.

words.definitionEn: a light with a single, very bright beam that can be directed at a particular place or person, especially a performer on the stage
words.exampleEn: The room was lit by spotlights.

words.definitionEn: a husband or wife
words.exampleEn: Fill in your spouse’s name here.

words.definitionEn: a person who tries to get secret information about another country, organization or person, especially somebody who is employed by a government or the police
words.exampleEn: He was denounced as a foreign spy.

words.definitionEn: to collect secret information about another country, organization or person
words.exampleEn: He spied for his government for more than ten years.

words.definitionEn: a section of a police force that deals with a particular type of crime
words.exampleEn: the drugs/fraud/bomb/riot squad

words.definitionEn: to press something, especially with your fingers
words.exampleEn: to squeeze a tube of toothpaste

words.definitionEn: to push a sharp, pointed object, especially a knife, into somebody, killing or injuring them
words.exampleEn: He was stabbed to death in a racist attack.

words.definitionEn: the quality or state of being steady and not changing or being upset in any way (= the quality of being stable)
words.exampleEn: political/economic/social stability

words.definitionEn: to become or to make something become steady and unlikely to change; to make something stable
words.exampleEn: The patient's condition stabilized.

words.definitionEn: the deliberate ending of a pregnancy at an early stage
words.exampleEn: to support/oppose abortion

words.definitionEn: money that somebody invests in a company
words.exampleEn: a 20 per cent stake in the business

words.definitionEn: done from a position in which you are standing rather than sitting or running
words.exampleEn: a standing jump/start

words.definitionEn: unpleasant; real, and impossible to avoid
words.exampleEn: The author paints a stark picture of life in a prison camp.

words.definitionEn: connected with or based on statistics
words.exampleEn: statistical analysis

words.definitionEn: to control the direction in which a boat, car, etc. moves
words.exampleEn: He steered the boat into the harbour.

words.definitionEn: the main long, thin part of a plant above the ground from which the leaves or flowers grow; a smaller part that grows from this and supports flowers or leaves
words.exampleEn: long, trailing stems of ivy

words.definitionEn: to stop something that is flowing from spreading or increasing
words.exampleEn: The cut was bandaged to stem the bleeding.

words.definitionEn: a fixed idea or image that many people have of a particular type of person or thing, but which is often not true in reality and may cause hurt and offence
words.exampleEn: cultural/gender/racial stereotypes

words.definitionEn: something that helps somebody/something to develop better or more quickly
words.exampleEn: stimulus for something, Books provide children with ideas and a stimulus for play.

words.definitionEn: to move a liquid or substance around, using a spoon or something similar, in order to mix it completely
words.exampleEn: stir something, She stirred her tea.

words.definitionEn: the process of keeping something in a particular place until it is needed; the space where things can be kept
words.exampleEn: tables that fold flat for storage

words.definitionEn: loved very much
words.exampleEn: in memory of our dearly beloved son, John

words.definitionEn: easy to do or to understand; not complicated
words.exampleEn: It's a relatively straightforward process.

words.definitionEn: pressure on a system or relationship because great demands are being placed on it
words.exampleEn: The transport service cannot cope with the strain of so many additional passengers.

words.definitionEn: a single thin piece of thread, wire, hair, etc.
words.exampleEn: He pulled at a loose strand of wool in his sweater.
